sampleBriefing.label: Sample Issue
sampleBriefing.headline: This week in the Valley
sampleBriefing.intro: Every Monday, we score 30+ RGV business stories by the <nri>Nolana Relevance Index</nri>. Here are sample top stories — Pro subscribers get the full briefing.
sampleBriefing.disclaimer: Sample format shown for demonstration. Real issues include direct source links to public records, city agendas, filings, and local media.

about.label: About
about.headline: Published by the people who know your numbers.
about.body: Published by <link>{publisher}</link>, a McAllen-based bookkeeping and tax coordination firm that works closely with Valley business owners. We built The Nolana Report to turn public business signals into a weekly briefing owners can actually use.
about.methodBody: Every week, we review public sources including city agendas, business filings, economic development updates, bridge wait data, FX movement, local media, and regional announcements. Stories are scored by local business relevance, urgency, and potential market impact using the Nolana Relevance Index.

faq.label: FAQ
faq.headline: Common questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about The Nolana Report.

items.question: What is The Nolana Report?
items.answer: The Nolana Report is a weekly business intelligence briefing for the Rio Grande Valley, covering new businesses, government moves, cross-border trade, and industrial investment across McAllen, Edinburg, Brownsville, and the RGV. Published every Monday by National Bookkeeping Company.

items.question: What business news sources cover the Rio Grande Valley?
items.answer: The Nolana Report aggregates and scores 25+ RGV news sources including KRGV, ValleyCentral, RGV Business Journal, The Monitor, Brownsville Herald, Rio Grande Guardian, Texas Border Business, and Valley Business Report, plus government data from the Federal Register, CBP bridge wait times, and USD/MXN exchange rates.

items.question: How much does The Nolana Report cost?
items.answer: The Nolana Report offers three tiers: Free (weekly summary email + live data bar), Pro at $9/month (full 30-story briefing with score badges and archive access), and Intel at $19/month (Pro features plus monthly deep-dive industry analysis). Founding members get Pro locked at $7/month forever.

items.question: What new businesses are opening in McAllen and the RGV?
items.answer: The Nolana Report tracks new business openings, LLC filings, permits, and expansions across McAllen, Edinburg, Pharr, Mission, Brownsville, Harlingen, and the entire Rio Grande Valley every week. Subscribe free at nolanareport.com to get the latest.

items.question: What companies are investing in the Rio Grande Valley?
items.answer: The Nolana Report's Industrial & Investment Watch tracks manufacturing plants, corporate relocations, Foreign Trade Zone activity, and economic development announcements across the RGV, including EDC news from Edinburg, McAllen, Weslaco, and the Port of Brownsville.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.collectBody: When you subscribe to The Nolana Report, we collect your email address and the date of your subscription. We do not collect payment information directly — billing is handled by Stripe.
privacy.useBody: Your email is used solely to deliver The Nolana Report newsletter and transactional emails related to your subscription (receipts, plan changes). We do not sell or share your email with third parties for marketing purposes.
privacy.thirdPartyIntro: We use the following services to operate the newsletter:
privacy.thirdPartyNote: Each service operates under its own privacy policy.
privacy.rightsBody: You may unsubscribe at any time by clicking the unsubscribe link in any newsletter email. To request deletion of your data, email us at
privacy.publisherBody: The Nolana Report is published by National Bookkeeping Company®, 315 W Nolana Ave, McAllen, TX 78504.
privacy.updatesBody: We may update this policy as the service evolves. Material changes will be communicated via newsletter.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.serviceBody: The Nolana Report is a weekly business intelligence newsletter covering the Rio Grande Valley. It is published by National Bookkeeping Company® and delivered to subscribers each Monday.
terms.tiersIntro: The newsletter is available on three tiers:
tiersItems: Intel ($19/mo) — Pro plus exclusive industrial and cross-border data
terms.tiersNote: Paid subscriptions are billed monthly through Stripe. You may cancel at any time; access continues through the end of the billing period.
terms.contentBody: Content in The Nolana Report is editorial in nature and provided for informational purposes only. It does not constitute legal, financial, or investment advice. Stories are sourced from public records, government filings, and local reporting; accuracy is not guaranteed and the publisher bears no liability for actions taken based on newsletter content.
terms.useBody: Subscribers may not redistribute, republish, or resell newsletter content without written permission. Sharing individual stories for personal, non-commercial purposes is permitted with attribution.
terms.cancelBody: You may cancel your paid subscription at any time. We do not offer prorated refunds for partial billing periods. To request a refund in exceptional circumstances, contact us at
terms.changesBody: We may update these terms as the service evolves. Continued subscription after notice of changes constitutes acceptance.

signals.label: Business Tip
signals.headline: Know something we should cover?
signals.subtitle: Saw a new store opening? Heard about a company moving in? Noticed construction on a new project? Tell us and we'll look into it for next Monday's briefing.
signals.whatPlaceholder: Construction crews showed up at the old Sears building on 10th. Looks like they're gutting the interior.
signals.namePrivacy: We never share who sent a tip.
signals.successBody: We'll look into this. If it checks out, you'll see it in a future Monday briefing.
signals.successContact: We have your contact on file if we need to follow up.
signals.disclaimer: Tips are reviewed before publication. Sending a tip does not guarantee it appears in the briefing.
